description: Add macOS CI jobs to the GitHub Actions CI workflow. macOS is Unix-like so the build works without modifications, but running CI on macos-latest catches regressions that Linux CI might miss (different filesystem case sensitivity, different Chrome behavior, different process signaling). Includes build + unit tests and E2E tests.

userStories.title: Add macOS build, unit test, and E2E job to CI workflow
userStories.description: Add a macOS job to the CI workflow that runs the platform build, unit tests, and E2E tests on macos-latest. macOS GitHub Actions runners have a real display so no xvfb-run is needed for headed Chromium.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'macos' job exists in .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs on macos-latest
The job runs: npm ci, npm run build, npm run test, npm run test:e2e
E2E tests run without xvfb-run (macOS has a real display)
Playwright report is uploaded as artifact on failure
The job does not depend on the Linux check job (runs in parallel)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (add macos job)

Context:
The CI workflow created by the github-actions-ci PRD has Linux jobs for checks, E2E, plugins, and docs, plus Windows jobs from the windows-ci PRD. This adds a macOS job.

macOS differences from Linux:
- No `xvfb-run` needed — macOS GitHub Actions runners have a real display (Aqua window server)
- Chrome extension E2E tests use `chromium.launchPersistentContext({ headless: false })` which needs a display
- macOS filesystem is case-insensitive by default (HFS+/APFS) vs Linux (ext4, case-sensitive) — this can surface import path bugs
- Process signals work the same as Linux (both POSIX)
- `chmod +x` works natively
- `sharp` has prebuilt binaries for darwin-arm64 and darwin-x64

Job structure:
```yaml
  macos:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    env:
      CI: '1'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Unit tests
        run: npm run test

      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install chromium --with-deps

      - name: Run E2E tests
        run: npm run test:e2e

      - name: Upload Playwright report
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-report-macos
          path: |
            playwright-report/
            test-results/
          retention-days: 7
```

Note: no `xvfb-run` wrapper — macOS runners have a GUI session. The E2E tests launch headed Chromium directly.

The macos-latest runner is arm64 (Apple Silicon M1) since November 2024. Node.js 22 and Playwright both support arm64 macOS natively.

We skip lint, format-check, and knip on macOS because those are platform-independent (Biome and knip produce identical results on all platforms). Build + unit tests + E2E is the sweet spot for catching macOS-specific issues.

Constraints:
- Do NOT add xvfb-run — macOS has a real display
- Do NOT duplicate lint/format/knip checks — those are platform-independent and already run on Linux
- The job runs in parallel with Linux and Windows jobs (no `needs` dependency)
- Use `macos-latest` (currently arm64 M1) — this matches the most common developer environment
- Artifact names must be unique across jobs — use `playwright-report-macos` not `playwright-report`
